definitions.researchCompleteness: Computed from exactly 8 fields: Country, Summary, Team Size, Sector, KPI Alignment, Direct Competitors, Differentiation, Sources. High = 6–8 valid fields. Medium = 3–5. Low = 0–2.
definitions.scoreability: Whether enough structured data exists to calculate a meaningful score. Four levels: scoreable_high (all 6 key fields), scoreable_medium (4–5), scoreable_low (2–3), not_scoreable (<2).
definitions.scoreConfidence: How reliable the resulting score is after normalization and judgment calls. High = minimal inference required. Medium = some fields inferred from context. Low = material missingness affects the score.
definitions.investorSignal: Qualitative investment-oriented conclusion used in the top-3 deep research layer. Derived from composite score, score confidence, and dossier analysis.
definitions.shortlist: Computed ranked top 20 from the five-pillar scoring model applied to the enriched company-only dataset.

benchmarkNote: The ranked output is benchmarked against the prior reference model, but the current shortlist is computed from the enriched dataset generated in this workflow.
disclaimer: This interface summarizes pipeline outputs. Source-backed evidence remains available in the memo, source register, and company dossiers.
